jurisdiction in their native state in the US. 
(d) Aadith was being manipulated and tutored against the Appellant 
by Respondent No. 4. Through this, Respondent No. 4 was 
actively trying to disobey the Court’s orders by restricting Aadith’s 
access to the Appellant. 
5. 
Per contra, Ms. Liz Mathew, Learned Senior Counsel, appearing 
on behalf of Respondent No. 4, put forth the following submissions: 
(a) Aadith is completely mentally fit to make decisions about his own 
welfare and well-being. Despite his limitations, he is capable 
of informed judgment and independent or supported decision-
making. He does not require a guardian by any means and 
needs only some support and assistance, which Respondent 
No. 4 has been duly providing. 
(b) Respondent No. 4 has never manipulated or coerced his son,
